predecessor_cycles.omega_runner_4: POC distroless image at 95.1 MB (-70 MB), 4/5 smoke PASS, B3 BLOCKED on popen('sh -c …')
predecessor_cycles.omega_runner_5: Added busybox-static (42 applets), 6/6 smoke PASS, recommended cutover with HIGH confidence

goal: Cut over docker/runner/Dockerfile (production) to the validated distroless+busybox layout, build new production image, restart hexa-exec container, smoke through resolver path and bridge_health.

2_dockerfile_swap.note: Dockerfile.distroless retained alongside Dockerfile for archive/comparison

3_production_dockerfile_amendments_for_m.issue_found: Original POC Dockerfile.distroless hard-coded /usr/lib/aarch64-linux-gnu/ COPY paths — multi-arch build (linux/amd64) failed with `lstat /usr/lib/aarch64-linux-gnu: no such file or directory` because the amd64 builder stage never has that arch dir.
3_production_dockerfile_amendments_for_m.fix: Added a builder-stage RUN that detects DEB_HOST_MULTIARCH at build time and stages the matching arch's .so files into /opt/runtime-libs/<arch_dir>/. Runtime stage now does two fixed-path COPYs (one per arch dir); only the matching arch's source dir is populated per buildx platform pass, so the other COPY is a no-op.
3_production_dockerfile_amendments_for_m.dockerfile_lines_changed_total: ~50 (replaced 32 explicit per-lib aarch64 COPYs with builder-stage stage-script + 2 wholesale COPYs)

4_production_dockerfile_amendment_for_re.issue_found: ~/.hx/bin/hexa resolver `_ensure_hexa_exec_container` invokes `docker run --entrypoint /bin/sleep "$DOCKER_IMAGE" infinity`. busybox apt install lays down sleep at /usr/bin/sleep but NOT /bin/sleep. First spawn after cutover failed with `stat /bin/sleep: no such file or directory` and the container was stuck in `Created` state.
4_production_dockerfile_amendment_for_re.fix: Added `ln -sf /bin/busybox /opt/bb/bin/sleep` to the busybox staging RUN — runtime gets /bin/sleep via the wholesale /opt/bb/bin/ → /bin/ COPY.
4_production_dockerfile_amendment_for_re.validated_with: docker run --rm --entrypoint /bin/sleep hexa-runner:latest 0.1 → exit 0; subsequent resolver-spawned hexa-exec → Status=Up

6_container_restart.before_rm: hexa-exec was Up with image_id 195721083d78 (cab8ef20 / pre-cutover legacy)

scenario_2_exec_date_via_resolver.note: Validates busybox `date` applet on /usr/bin/date is reachable via popen('sh -c …').

scenario_3_file_io_via_resolver.note: Validates write+read+pipe through busybox sh + cat + printf applets.

scenario_5_bin_sleep_resolvable.note: Required by resolver for long-lived hexa-exec container.

bridge_health_post_cutover.marker: __BRIDGE_HEALTH__ PASS total=16 pass=16 fail=0 tampered=0 duration_ms=30000
bridge_health_post_cutover.interpretation: All curl-based bridge endpoints reachable from inside the new distroless+busybox image. /usr/bin/curl with all transitive .so deps wired correctly via the multi-arch /opt/runtime-libs staging.

savings_realized.actual_better_than_predicted_reason: Replaced 32 per-lib aarch64 COPYs (each pulled in its own layer overhead + the missing libldap_r-2.4 was a no-op COPY in arm64-only POC) with a single staged dir. Image rebuild also dropped some inert layers from the POC.

trigger_conditions: Any hexa workload that previously worked under hexa-runner:cab8ef20 fails after cutover
Container fails to start or repeatedly exits
steps: 2. cd /Users/ghost/core/hexa-lang && hexa tool/build_runner_image.hexa  # rebuilds 165MB legacy image, retags :latest
3. docker rm -f hexa-exec  # resolver respawns from new :latest on next call
4. Re-run smoke + bridge_health to confirm green
rollback_recipe.notes: Backup Dockerfile retained at docker/runner/Dockerfile.bak.pre-runner6. Distroless POC source retained at docker/runner/Dockerfile.distroless for reference (matches new production verbatim minus the multi-arch lib staging amendment + /bin/sleep symlink).

post_cutover_issues_observed.issue: POC Dockerfile.distroless hard-coded aarch64-only lib COPY paths; multi-arch build failed
post_cutover_issues_observed.resolution: Added builder-stage arch-aware staging RUN + two wholesale COPYs (see cutover step 3)

post_cutover_issues_observed.issue: Resolver expects /bin/sleep but busybox only laid it at /usr/bin/sleep — first hexa-exec spawn was stuck in Created state
First smoke attempt used hexa stdlib file_open/file_write APIs which are not currently registered (Runtime error: undefined function: file_open).
post_cutover_issues_observed.resolution: Reworked smoke to use exec_with_status('printf … > … && cat …') — pre-existing hexa runtime gap, unrelated to cutover

registry_promotion_eligibility.recommendation: PROMOTE — production cutover is green end-to-end.
